nodes.context: Boston-based Chinese criminal organization active from 1970s to early 2000s, involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, fraud, prostitution, human trafficking, illegal gambling, money laundering, extortion, murder, illegal immigration, and racketeering.
Hong Kong-based triad, one of the largest Chinese organized crime groups globally.
New York City-based Chinese criminal organization, allied with Ping On in the late 1980s.

edges.context: Ping On was regarded as a 'farm team' for 14K, and its founder had heavy ties to 14K, though not directly affiliated.
Ping On had several allies in the late 1980s, including Hung Mong in New York City.
